race.name: Payakentsut
race.tagline: Fifty kilometers on Olympic rings — where 2010 legacy trails meet Coast Mountain grandeur in the Sea-to-Sky corridor.

climate.description: Whistler Olympic Park sits at 850m elevation in the Callaghan Valley. February temperatures range from -10°C to 2°C. Heavy Pacific snowfall ensures deep snowpack, but proximity to the coast means occasional warm intrusions and wet snow. The Callaghan Valley is more sheltered and colder than Whistler Village.
challenges: Pacific warm fronts can bring rain or wet, heavy snow
Altitude gain from valley floor adds temperature and snow variability
Klister conditions possible in warm spells
Heavy snowfall can dump 30cm+ overnight, softening the track

course.primary: Courses use the 2010 Winter Olympic cross-country ski venue in the Callaghan Valley. World-class groomed trails at altitude wind through Coast Mountain old-growth forest with significant climbing and technical descents.
course.grooming: Olympic venue standard — among the best groomed trails in North America
features: 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympics cross-country ski venue
Callaghan Valley — quieter and more pristine than Whistler Village
Views of surrounding glaciated peaks on clear days

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Olympic-legacy grooming and spectacular Coast Mountain scenery are the standouts. Relatively young event (2011) limits prestige, but the venue infrastructure is world-class. Perfect grooming and scenery scores.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: TIER 2 — Olympic infrastructure and mountain beauty elevate a young race

history.summary: Founded in 2011, Payakentsut was created to activate the 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympics cross-country ski venue as a citizen race destination. The Whistler Olympic Park in the Callaghan Valley features world-class trail infrastructure built for Olympic competition. The race name honors the indigenous heritage of the region. Despite its youth, the event benefits from Olympic-level grooming, timing, and venue management.
notable_facts: Uses the 2010 Vancouver Winter Olympics cross-country ski venue
Whistler Olympic Park is operated by the Resort Municipality of Whistler
The Callaghan Valley is separate from Whistler Village — quieter and more remote
Olympic biathlon and cross-country events were held on these trails
One of the youngest major marathon ski events in Canada
The venue regularly hosts national-level and World Cup junior competitions
